2604.08169.title: Activation Steering for Aligned Open-ended Generation without Sacrificing Coherence
2604.08169.paper_summary: Alignment in LLMs is more brittle than commonly assumed: misalignment can be triggered by adversarial prompts, benign fine-tuning, emergent misalignment, and goal misgeneralization. Recent evidence suggests that some misalignment behaviors are encoded as linear structure in activation space, making it tractable via steering, while safety alignment has been shown to govern the first few output tokens primarily, leaving subsequent generation unguarded. These findings motivate activation steering as a lightweight runtime defense that continuously corrects misaligned activations throughout generation. We evaluate three methods: Steer-With-Fixed-Coeff (SwFC), which applies uniform additive steering, and two novel projection-aware methods, Steer-to-Target-Projection (StTP) and Steer-to-Mirror-Projection (StMP), that use a logistic regression decision boundary to selectively intervene only on tokens whose activations fall below distributional thresholds. Using malicious system prompts as a controlled proxy for misalignment, we evaluate under two threat models (dishonesty and dismissiveness) and two architectures (Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, Qwen3-32B). All methods substantially recover target traits (honesty and compassion) while preserving coherence. StTP and StMP better maintain general capabilities (MMLU, MT-Bench, AlpacaEval) and produce less repetition in multi-turn conversations.
2604.08169.paper_summary_zh: LLMs中的對齊比一般假設的更脆弱：不對齊可以由對抗性提示、無害的微調、新興的不對齊和目標的錯誤概括引發。最近的證據表明，一些不對齊行為在激活空間中被編碼為線性結構，使其可以通過引導進行處理，而安全對齊主要控制前幾個輸出標記，留下後續生成未受保護。這些發現促使了激活引導作為一種輕量級的運行時防禦，持續修正生成過程中的不對齊激活。我們評估了三種方法：Steer-With-Fixed-Coeff (SwFC)，它應用均勻的加性引導，以及兩種新的投影感知方法，Steer-to-Target-Projection (StTP)和Steer-to-Mirror-Projection (StMP)，這些方法使用邏輯回歸決策邊界來選擇性地干預僅在激活低於分佈閾值的標記。使用惡意系統提示作為不對齊的控制代理，我們在兩種威脅模型（不誠實和輕視）和兩種架構（Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct，Qwen3-32B）下進行評估。所有方法在保持一致性的同時，顯著恢復目標特徵（誠實和同情心）。StTP和StMP更好地維持一般能力（MMLU，MT-Bench，AlpacaEval），並在多輪對話中產生更少的重複。
2604.08169.author: Niklas Herbster et.al.
2604.08169.authors: Niklas Herbster, Martin Zborowski, Alberto Tosato, Gauthier Gidel, Tommaso Tosato
